behaviors.description: The evaluate-stage command stores grounding into a dedicated `grounding` artifact, not the `plan` artifact.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is command-orchestration plus artifact-store handoff (not a single pure function). MOCK-MISS: wrong slot write appears when evaluate wiring calls persistence. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: no real OS/network/subprocess required. LLM-DEP: behavior checks destination key, not model judgment quality. Rejected System because behavior text is only about artifact slot routing; rejected Agentic/Workflow because correctness does not depend on model output content or multi-agentic decision chain.
behaviors.test_description: Run evaluate-stage with in-process artifact store and a deterministic model stub; assert `grounding` is written and `plan` is untouched by evaluate.

behaviors.description: The implement-stage flow reads grounding when available and still writes its execution output to `plan`.
behaviors.justification: This is cross-module state handoff: implement reads one artifact and writes another. Unit is too low because bug appears at read/write wiring boundary. No real infra dependency. Rejected Agentic because behavior is about using available grounding artifact and output slot selection, not evaluating model output quality; rejected Workflow because only one stage’s behavior is asserted.
behaviors.test_description: Seed store with `grounding`, execute implement flow with deterministic upstream inputs, assert grounding is consumed (e.g., appears in prompt/context payload) and final write target is `plan`.

behaviors.description: Deep-dive no longer overwrites `plan` as a redundant side effect when diagnosis already exists in issue content.
behaviors.justification: Real failure is interaction between issue-content inspection logic and artifact persistence side effects. Unit-only check can miss an actual write call in orchestrated flow. No OS/network/subprocess needed. Rejected Agentic because the predicate is presence of diagnosis in issue content, not quality of model reasoning; rejected System due no real infrastructure requirement.
behaviors.test_description: Provide issue content containing diagnosis, run deep-dive stage end-to-end with in-process store, assert no new `plan` write occurs and existing artifacts remain unchanged.

behaviors.description: In a full multi-step agent run (`deep-dive -> write-plan/evaluate -> implement`), the final implementation plan reflects grounding produced earlier in the run.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires multi-step sequential pipeline correctness across stages. MULTI-STEP applies directly: deep-dive/evaluate/implement chain and cross-step state carryover. Integration would under-test because it can pass with stitched stubs while missing failures in stage-to-stage agentic handoff semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the real chained run with all three stages enabled; verify grounding created earlier is present in implement-stage context and materially reflected in final `plan` artifact content.

behaviors.description: When grounding is absent, implement falls back safely and still emits a valid plan artifact without corrupting other stored artifacts.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is fallback orchestration with artifact-store state safety (read-miss + write + non-target preservation). Unit is too low because corruption risk is in module interaction. Rejected System: no real OS/network/subprocess needed. Rejected Agentic: correctness is fallback/control-path and artifact integrity, not model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run implement with no `grounding` present in store; assert `plan` is still produced, no exceptions, and non-plan artifacts are byte-for-byte unchanged.
